input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>

- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.

queries.question: What text overlay appears on the screen claiming that choosing vegetables over meat is a healthier option? A. Many people don't know that they don't have to always eat meat to get protein into their diet B. Besides meat, there are a number of vegetables that you can eat C. Moreover, the choice of vegetables over meat is more healthier D. as it reduces the risk of diabetes, heart disease and even some types of cancers

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A white screen displays centered blue text "Diet Plans And Weight Loss Programs Visit :" above a green grass icon and the URL "Serious-Fitness-Programs.com". [2-14] The scene shifts to a slide with the URL in the top left. On the right, a blonde woman in a blue shirt stands behind a table, using tongs to mix a green salad in a glass bowl; a bottle of dressing and red vegetables sit nearby. Large yellow text on the left changes sequentially. [2-6] The text reads: "Many people don't know that they don't have to always eat meat to get protein into their diet". [6-10] The text updates to: "Besides meat, there are a number of vegetables that you can eat". [10-14] The final text displays: "to get just right amount of protein you need to help you lose weight faster". [14-16] Large yellow text on the left states "Moreover, the choice of vegetables over meat is more healthier". To the right, a cartoon blonde woman in a blue top mixes a green salad in a glass bowl with wooden tongs, beside a bottle of dressing and red peppers. [16-18] The text on the left fades away completely, leaving a white

[52-54] The slide transitions as green pea graphics vanish, replaced by an orange 'Chickpeas' label at the bottom left and a pile of yellow-orange chickpeas on the right, under the red '9 High Protein Vegetables' banner. [54-56] Large beige text fades in on the left: 'This is a vegetable that can be added to salads'. [56-60] The text updates to: 'blending them into a hummus and having them baked in the oven with a little buffalo sauce'. [60-62] The text changes to: 'They contain 8 grams of protein per serving'. [62-64] Finally, the text reads: 'and it's a good way to get just the right amount of protein you need for the day'. Throughout, the chickpea illustration and labels remain static.

[64-68] Under the persistent red header "9 High Protein Vegetables", beige text reads "and it's a good way to get just the right amount of protein you need for the day". An orange "Chickpeas" label and a pile of yellow chickpeas illustrations occupy the bottom right. Brown text "Potatoes" appears centered at the very top. [68-74] The scene transitions to show a brown sack filled with yellow potatoes on the left side, with "POTATOES" printed on the bag. A large brown "Potatoes" label appears at the bottom center. Beige text fades in on the right side, stating "Potatoes are usually not eaten alone and this increases their value". The red header remains visible at the top throughout the sequence.

From 76-78s, a brown sack filled with yellow potatoes labeled "POTATOES" is positioned on the left. Beige text on the right states "They are a favorite with many individuals," located under the red header "9 High Protein Vegetables" and above a brown "Potatoes" label. From 78-80s, the beige text updates to "but most of us don't realize how much protein it contains" while the sack and labels remain static. From 80-82s, the text changes to "you can get 5 grams of protein from just a medium sized potato". At 82-84s, the scene shifts to a new slide featuring a green cartoon broccoli character with a face and flexed arms on the left against a teal background. The bottom label changes to light blue "Broccoli". From 84-86s, new beige text appears on the right reading "A cup of broccoli contains 3 grams of protein and it's a good source of fiber as well", while the red header persists.

[86-94] A slide titled "9 High Protein Vegetables" features a green cartoon broccoli character flexing on a teal background (left) and beige text on a white background (right). Initially, the text reads "A cup of broccoli contains 3 grams of protein and it's a good source of fiber as well" with a "Broccoli" label below. At 88s, the text updates to "You can add it to almost anything If you want a real treat". This remains until 92s, when the text changes to "try roasting broccoli in the oven with extra virgin oil and some garlic". The character and header remain static throughout.

[94-96] The slide remains static, displaying a green cartoon broccoli character flexing muscles on the left against a teal background. Beige text on the right reads "try roasting broccoli in the oven with extra virgin oil and some garlic". The red header and blue bottom label persist unchanged.

[96-98] The green cartoon broccoli character flexing muscles on the left remains static against the teal background. The beige text on the right updates to read "then sprinkle it with fresh parmesan cheese after taking it out of the oven". The red header and blue bottom label persist unchanged.

queries.question: What text overlay appears on the screen claiming that choosing vegetables over meat is a healthier option? A. Many people don't know that they don't have to always eat meat to get protein into their diet B. Besides meat, there are a number of vegetables that you can eat C. Moreover, the choice of vegetables over meat is more healthier D. as it reduces the risk of diabetes, heart disease and even some types of cancers

samples.output: <think>The green cartoon broccoli character flexing muscles remains static on the left against a teal background. On the right, the beige instructional text regarding parmesan cheese fades away, becoming transparent and disappearing from the white background, while the red header and blue label persist.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: What text overlay appears on the screen claiming that choosing vegetables over meat is a healthier option? A. Many people don't know that they don't have to always eat meat to get protein into their diet B. Besides meat, there are a number of vegetables that you can eat C. The red header "9 High Protein Vegetables" and green "Corn" label remain visible.</think><answer></answer>

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[42-44] Slide transitions. Left text vanishes. Green "Peas" label bottom left. Right side has light green background with open pea pods illustration under "GREEN PEAS".

[46-48] Left text changes to "Each serving of peas will give you 8 grams of protein". Green "Peas" label and right graphic remain.

From 84-86s, new beige text appears on the right reading "A cup of broccoli contains 3 grams of protein and it's a good source of fiber as well", while the red header persists.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
